entity.summary: Wallet-integrated non-custodial exchange built on NEO that launched on mainnet in October 2018, temporarily halted trading after regulatory and market concerns, relaunched with KYC, and ultimately announced closure in October 2019 after low usage and sustained operating losses.
entity.notes: HEI treats the October 8, 2019 operator-announced wind-down as the terminal date and voluntary_shutdown as the primary death reason. Regulatory uncertainty contributed to the November 2018 pause, but the final announcement emphasized low usage, operating losses, and an unsustainable project. The 2018-10-10 date is the NEO MainNet DEX launch, not the earlier wallet or TestNet release.

events.title: Aphelion DEX launched on NEO MainNet
events.description: Aphelion opened its wallet-integrated decentralized exchange for NEO MainNet trading.
events.notes: The date is confirmed by an official first-week update and contemporary NEO ecosystem reporting.

events.title: MainNet trading was disabled
events.description: Aphelion disabled all MainNet DEX market pairs while citing new SEC rulings, market conditions, NEO technical challenges, and diminishing cash flow.
events.notes: This was a temporary halt rather than the final shutdown.

events.title: Aphelion DEX returned with KYC controls
events.description: Aphelion announced that trading had returned after adding identity-verification controls intended to exclude United States participants.
events.notes: The first-party announcement is dated January 1, 2019.

events.title: Aphelion announced exchange closure
events.description: Aphelion said it was taking steps to close the non-custodial exchange after low usage, operating losses, and weak token economics made the project unsustainable.
events.notes: No verified exchange restart followed the operator's closure announcement.

evidence.title: Aphelion Wallet/DEX Issues First Update Since Initial Mainnet Release
evidence.notes: First-party update describes the first week after the NEO MainNet launch.

evidence.title: Aphelion DEX slated for NEO MainNet launch on October 10th
evidence.notes: Contemporary ecosystem reporting records the October 10 MainNet launch schedule.

evidence.title: Aphelion Disables Mainnet DEX—What it means and where do we go from here?
evidence.notes: First-party notice states that all market pairs would be disabled and lists the regulatory, market, technical, and cash-flow factors.

evidence.title: Aphelion DEX is Officially Back — Trading Live Now
evidence.notes: First-party relaunch announcement describes restored trading and KYC restrictions.

evidence.title: Aphelion announces it is shutting down its non-custodial exchange
evidence.notes: Detailed contemporary report links the official closure announcement and preserves the operator's stated reasons for ending the exchange.
